title: toxicodendron trichocarpum f. viride
intro: Toxicodendron trichocarpum f. viride, also known as the poison oak, is a deciduous shrub or vine native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant contains a toxin called urushiol, which causes an allergic reaction in humans, leading to the characteristic rash and blisters associated with poison oak exposure. Despite its toxic nature, some herbal preparations of poison oak have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including arthritis and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of poison oak are thought to include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and herbal preparations may involve drying and processing the plant to reduce the urushiol content.
